nativeRange.habitat: Small, slow-moving freshwater streams and pools under tropical moist forest canopy; the only purely freshwater fish native to the granitic Seychelles (Mahé, Praslin, Silhouette, La Digue). An introduced population formerly existed on Zanzibar but has been largely extirpated by invasive tilapia.

decorPreferences: dense surface vegetation and floating plants (water sprite, Amazon frogbit, hornwort)
submerged plant thickets for cover and spawning sites
driftwood and leaf litter to diffuse light and lower pH naturally
tight-fitting lid with no gaps — killifish are strong jumpers
dark substrate to reduce stress and intensify male coloration

diet.notes: An unfussy surface predator. Accepts quality dried foods (flakes, micro-pellets) readily, but thrives with regular live or frozen supplements: bloodworm, Daphnia, Tubifex, Artemia, and small insect larvae. In the wild feeds on worms, crustaceans, insects, and small fish. Will consume fry and nano fish that fit in its mouth.

goodWith: other similarly-sized killifish of different genera
avoidWith: nano fish and fish under 2–3 cm (predation risk)
dwarf shrimp and small invertebrates (will be eaten)
other Pachypanchax males without ample hiding space (territorial fighting)
Blue Panchax (Aplocheilus panchax fw-fish-157) — different genus and origin, frequently misidentified as this species in trade

imageCandidates.notes: Male specimen showing characteristic reddish-brown flank spots and iridescent yellow-green flanks. Good representative image.

summary: Playfair’s Panchax is the only freshwater fish endemic to the granitic Seychelles — a non-annual killifish reaching ~10 cm, hardy and easy to keep, but a confirmed surface jumper requiring a sealed lid. Not to be confused with the Asian Blue Panchax (Aplocheilus panchax), a different genus entirely.
careNotes: Playfair’s Panchax (Pachypanchax playfairii) is a robust, non-annual killifish and one of the easiest killifish to keep in the aquarium. Unlike many killifish that are tied to seasonal drying pools, P. playfairii inhabits permanent forest streams in the granitic Seychelles and lives several years (typically 3–5). This means eggs do not require a drying/diapause period, and the fish itself is far more tolerant of suboptimal conditions than annual killi species. A tank of at least 80 L with a minimum 60 cm footprint is recommended for a pair or small group; the tank must have a secure, tight-fitting lid with no gaps — like essentially all killifish, this species is a skilled jumper and escape deaths are common in uncovered tanks. This is explicitly flagged as high escape risk. The species is a surface-oriented ambush predator and prefers a heavily planted setup with floating cover and subdued lighting; the dark flanks-and-spots male coloration is best appreciated over a dark substrate. Water chemistry is flexible (pH 6.0–8.0, gH 5–19, temperature 20–26°C), making it accessible to beginners. Males are territorial toward each other; a single male with one or two females is the recommended social unit unless the tank is large and heavily decorated. Any fish small enough to fit in its mouth — including dwarf shrimp and nano fish — will eventually be eaten. P. playfairii is frequently misidentified as or confused with the Blue Panchax (Aplocheilus panchax, fw-fish-157): the two belong to different genera (Pachypanchax vs. Aplocheilus), originate on different continents (Indian Ocean islands vs. South and Southeast Asia), and are not closely related despite superficial similarity. IUCN status is Data Deficient (2016); the Zanzibar introduction has been largely lost to invasive tilapia, and the species’ entire wild range is restricted to a few small Seychellois islands.
breedingNotes: Playfair’s Panchax is a moderately easy, non-annual egg-scatterer. Spawning occurs among fine-leaved plants, floating vegetation, and Java moss mops over a period of 8–14 days, with 50–200 eggs deposited per spawning event. Eggs hatch in 10–12 days at 24°C. Parents are confirmed egg-eaters; for best yield, move eggs to a separate rearing container or use dense, sacrificial plant cover to allow some fry to avoid predation in the main tank. Fry are tiny at hatching and require infusoria or freshly-hatched Artemia nauplii as first foods, graduating to micro-worms and finely crushed flake as they grow. A dedicated breeding tank of ~30 L with dense planting, one male, and two females at 24–26°C is the standard approach. No diapause or substrate conditioning is needed — this is a key advantage over annual killi species for beginners.

primary.name: SeriouslyFish — Pachypanchax playfairii
primary.notes: Primary source. Provided temperature 18–24°C, pH 6.0–8.0, hardness 90–268 ppm (~5–19 dH), adult size 90–100 mm SL, native range (granitic Seychelles: Mahé, Praslin, Silhouette, La Digue; translocated Zanzibar), habitat (small forest streams), diet (earthworms, bloodworm, Tubifex, Daphnia, gut-loaded insects), breeding method (egg scatterer among plants/mosses), and social behavior (males territorial but rarely seriously injure rivals). Temperature upper bound extended to 26°C per corroborating sources.

additional.name: FishBase — Pachypanchax playfairii
additional.notes: Cross-checked adult size (10 cm SL max), temperature 22–24°C, pH 6.0–8.0, hardness 5–19 dH, taxonomy (Aplocheilidae, Cyprinodontiformes), diet (carnivore: worms, crustaceans, insects, fish), trophic level 3.6, reproduction (50–200 eggs, non-migratory), IUCN Data Deficient status. Confirmed Africa / Seychelles endemic. Note: FishBase lists the species as occurring in freshwater and brackish streams and ponds, though SeriouslyFish describes it as the only purely freshwater native of the Seychelles; salinity set to null (primarily freshwater).

additional.name: Wikipedia — Pachypanchax playfairii
additional.notes: Confirmed sole member of Pachypanchax outside Madagascar; described by Günther 1866; first killifish in the European hobby (France, 1867); Zanzibar introduction now largely extinct due to invasive tilapia; lifespan “three years is common” for non-annual species. Also noted male breeding behavior (raised scales).

additional.name: Tropical Freshwater Fish — Pachypanchax playfairi
additional.notes: Supplementary source. Tank dimensions (60 cm / 38–56 L), temperature 23–25°C, pH 6.5–7.0, hardness 5–15 dH, spawning duration 8–14 days, egg hatching 10–12 days, compatibility list (corydoras, West African dwarf cichlids, hatchetfish). Parents described as “notorious egg-eaters.” Care difficulty rated 3 (hardy); breeding difficulty 5 (fairly easy).

additional.name: Diszhal.info — Pachypanchax playfairii
additional.notes: Additional care data: minimum tank 80 L, temperature 23–25°C (breeding 24–26°C), pH 6.5–7.0, hardness 5–15 NK°, spawning duration ~1 week, egg incubation 12 days at 24°C. Confirmed surface jumper requiring well-covered tank. Lifespan stated as 1–2 years in this source — likely underestimates wild/optimal-care lifespan; Wikipedia and non-annual killifish biology support 3–5 years; 3–5 used.

fish.breedingNotes: Non-annual egg-scatterer; spawns among plants and mops over 8–14 days, 50–200 eggs per clutch, hatch in 10–12 days at 24°C. No diapause required. Parents eat eggs; separate eggs or use dense plant cover. Fry fed infusoria then Artemia nauplii.
